entry.text: He comes around the corner at a run, slightly out of breath, waving both arms. He is bald, old, and heavily scarred in a way that suggests a long career of specifically bad decisions. 'STOP,' he says, and you do. 'There is a trap ahead. Major trap. Spring-loaded, chest height, very bad.' He points down the alley you were planning to use. 'I know how to get around it. But you'll need a ward of protection first.' He produces, from somewhere in his jacket, a sealed plastic bag. 'This ward,' he says, 'will keep you safe from the trap. It's five scrap. Firm price.'

choices.label: Pay the five scrap and take the ward
Push past him — you don't trust it

ward_received.text: He hands you the bag. Inside: a pair of clean white underwear, sealed in original packaging, with a pre-war brand logo still legible on the front. He nods seriously. 'The ward protects. You'll be fine now.' Then he runs away, laughing, at a speed that is alarming for his apparent age. The underwear does nothing. It is, however, spotlessly clean.

no_trap_found.text: You walk the alley top to bottom. There is no trap. There was never a trap. There is, however, a small pile of scrap in the corner that suggests other people have used this route recently, possibly after paying him. You take what's there.

ward_worthless.text: He drops the serious face for exactly half a second before it reassembles. You handed over the scrap. You know it. He knows it. The transaction has concluded.

actual_trap.text: There is, improbably, an actual trap. Spring-loaded, chest height, very bad. The old man was not lying, which is not the outcome you prepared for.
